description: This figure presents a parity plot (scatter diagram) comparing predicted versus experimental solid solution strengthening stress (Δσ_SSS) values for Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel samples using a Support Vector Machine (SVR) model. The circular data points represent 'All Samples' and are color-coded by prediction error magnitude (ranging from 0.60 MPa in dark blue to 29.90 MPa in dark red according to the vertical color bar). A dashed 'Guide line' indicates the ideal 1:1 correlation where predictions exactly match experimental values. The plot axes span 1200–1800 MPa for both experimental (x-axis) and predicted (y-axis) Δσ_SSS. Key model performance metrics are annotated directly on the figure: RMSE of 0.31 and R² of 0.89, indicating extreme high prediction accuracy. The tight clustering of data points along the diagonal guideline demonstrates that the SVR model, incorporating modified solid solution strengthening theory features (shear modulus G, cohesive energy difference δ_Ec, and local cohesive energy mismatch D_Ec), reliably predicts the strengthening contribution across the arc-cladded steel dataset, with the majority of predictions showing errors below 15 MPa.
